description: Machine-readable index of the AI agent harness. Human spec: HARNESS.md, loop protocol: LOOPS.md.

promptPack.title: Understand
promptPack.deliverable: Component/layer impact map and graph freshness status.
instructions: Load the understand-process skill before analysis.
Run the graph freshness gate and document whether it is up to date or blocked.
Map changed components, one-hop affected components, affected layers, and hotspots.
Record missing context and reduced-confidence assumptions explicitly.

architect.output: Architecture Brief (scope, artifacts, decisions, constraints, validation, assumptions)

promptPack.title: Architect
promptPack.deliverable: Architecture Brief with gate decisions, files, constraints, validation, and assumptions.
instructions: Read understand-notes.md first.
Run gates 1-5 (and gate 4b when applicable) with explicit pass/fail reasoning.
Define exactly which files or artifacts change and why they belong there.
Persist the settled brief in the repository memory briefs folder when implementation proceeds.

architect-challenge.output: VERDICT: APPROVED | REVISE on the Architecture Brief

promptPack.title: Architect Challenge
promptPack.deliverable: Independent challenge verdict on the Architecture Brief.
instructions: Read architecture-brief.md before judging the plan.
Approve, revise, or block with explicit evidence and gate reasoning.
Escalate any guardrail, approval, or missing-context issue that would make implementation unsafe.

requiredFor: overriding a blocked architectural risk without human sign-off

implement.output: delivered change + proof summary + self-review summary

promptPack.title: Implement
promptPack.deliverable: Applied changes plus proof summary and self-review notes.
instructions: Read architecture-brief.md and stay within its ownership boundaries.
Load relevant domain skills before editing.
Complete pre-implementation discovery, then apply the smallest rooted change.
Record validation commands, proof artifacts, and self-review outcomes.

review-breadth.output: findings ledger (severity, evidence, impact, confidence, fix)

promptPack.title: Review Breadth
promptPack.deliverable: Findings ledger for correctness, standards, safety, and proof quality.
instructions: Read architecture-brief.md and implementation-notes.md.
Review by lanes and list findings first, ordered by severity, with concrete file references.
Check harness/tooling claims against shipped repo capability surfaces when relevant.

requiredFor: accepting a missing approval boundary as non-blocking
accepting unsupported capability claims without evidence

review-depth.output: gate ledger + structural findings + Brief divergences

promptPack.title: Review Depth
promptPack.deliverable: Gate ledger and structural findings checked against the Architecture Brief.
instructions: Read architecture-brief.md, implementation-notes.md, and review-breadth-findings.md.
Re-run the architectural gates against the implemented diff.
Trace ownership, boundaries, systemic risks, and capability claims.

requiredFor: reducing a safety guardrail to accept the structure

feedback.output: verdict record + Brief updates + response notes

promptPack.title: Feedback
promptPack.deliverable: Verdict record, response notes, and any Brief updates.
instructions: Read architecture-brief.md plus both review outputs.
Produce a point-by-point verdict record with accepted, rejected, deferred, or changed decisions.
Update the brief if decisions changed and refresh next-steps with what remains.

requiredFor: changing destructive defaults without human acceptance

optimizer.description: DSPy-based skill optimizer for instruction improvement via iterative refinement

mcpTools.name: harness:mcp:find
mcpTools.description: Locate components affected by changes (read-only search)
mcpTools.outputs: file path, line number, match type, code excerpt

mcpTools.name: harness:mcp:impact
mcpTools.description: Analyze blast radius of changes (transitive dependency analysis)

scenarios.input: I have a feature idea. How do I design where the code should live?
scenarios.explanation: Shows Architecture Brief creation with gate analysis, constraints, validation, and assumptions

scenarios.input: Should this logic go in the service or controller?
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates gate analysis for correct layer placement

scenarios.input: I have an Architecture Brief. How do I implement the changes?
scenarios.explanation: Shows implementation packet review, proof selection, and self-review summary

scenarios.input: How do I validate my implementation before submitting for review?
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates proof-summary expectations and deterministic validation before review

scenarios.input: I need to do a first-pass code review. What should I check?
scenarios.explanation: Shows breadth review lanes, evidence-backed severity, and proof-quality checks

scenarios.input: How do I categorize issues by severity in a code review?
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates findings-ledger severity with evidence, impact, confidence, and fixes

scenarios.input: How do I verify that a service is in the right domain?
scenarios.explanation: Shows gate-ledger analysis for ownership, boundaries, reuse, and Brief conformance

scenarios.input: How do I check for cross-tenant data leakage?
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates multi-tenant isolation checks and tenant-scoped query verification

scenarios.input: A reviewer challenged my service placement. How do I respond?
scenarios.explanation: Shows point-by-point adjudication using the Brief, findings, and gate evidence

scenarios.input: I got feedback about my Architecture Brief. Should I revise?
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates verdict-record decisions, Brief updates, and response-note generation

scenarios.input: How do I save a hard-won debugging lesson so future sessions do not rediscover it?
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates using remember skill to persist lessons to .github/harness/memory/

scenarios.input: I found the root cause of a production issue. How do I document it for the team?
scenarios.explanation: Shows persisting Architecture Briefs and lessons with proper formatting

scenarios.input: My build is failing. How do I use the build-fix loop?
scenarios.explanation: Shows bounded iteration: compile → analyze → fix → recompile until green

scenarios.input: Tests are failing in CI. How do I drive the test-fix loop?
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates test iteration with root cause analysis before each fix

scenarios.input: I need to understand the blast radius of a database change before implementing.
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates graph-first discovery and dependency impact analysis

scenarios.input: What files are affected if I rename a core service?
scenarios.explanation: Shows how to trace cross-domain dependencies and usage patterns

scenarios.input: How do we convert domain knowledge into machine-operational guidance for agents?
scenarios.explanation: Shows lifecycle-gated guidance authoring with provenance and contradiction checks

scenarios.input: What must pass before candidate knowledge can teach agents in production workflows?
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates promotion gates, ownership boundaries, and agent-centric evaluation criteria

scenarios.input: I found a new AI technique. How do I track and evaluate it?
scenarios.explanation: Shows logging techniques, setting evaluation criteria, and adoption triaging

scenarios.input: Should we adopt this new vector search approach?
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates technique evaluation and integration decisions

scenarios.input: I am switching to a new task. How do I preserve context?
scenarios.explanation: Shows session memory saving and task-switch checkpoint creation

scenarios.input: My output quality is declining. Is it a context issue?
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates context inventory analysis and refresh patterns

scenarios.input: How do I prove that my code changes are complete?
scenarios.explanation: Shows objective proof methods and phase exit criteria

scenarios.input: Tests fail but I think implementation is correct. What now?
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates re-plan on failure: identify root cause, never skip checks

scenarios.input: Should I adopt this new library or stick with the current approach?
scenarios.explanation: Shows baseline establishment and comparative evaluation methodology

scenarios.input: How do I measure if an optimization actually improves our system?
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates adoption readiness checks and metric selection

scenarios.input: I need to change encryption keys. How do I ensure zero data loss?
scenarios.explanation: Shows high-stakes patterns: assumption checks, reversibility analysis

scenarios.input: I want a security expert to review my auth change before deploy.
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates cross-model review for production security changes

scenarios.input: How do I add Application Insights telemetry to my service?
scenarios.explanation: Shows Winston logging, Application Insights SDK setup, and custom metrics

scenarios.input: I need to debug a production issue. What telemetry should I check?
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates correlation IDs, structured logging, and trace analysis

scenarios.input: How do I ensure my implementation stays within token budget?
scenarios.explanation: Shows token counting, context sizing, and efficient tool use

scenarios.input: I am running out of tokens. What should I prioritize?
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates budget triage and essential-only task sequencing

scenarios.input: How do I initialize the harness for a new project?
scenarios.explanation: Shows harness setup: registry, skills tree, workflows, validation

scenarios.input: What documentation should I set up for issue-tracking mode?
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates issue template setup, label configuration, and routing

scenarios.input: My feature is ready. How do I open a pull request?
scenarios.explanation: Shows PR creation, branch naming, commit message conventions

scenarios.input: How do I request a Copilot review before human review?
scenarios.explanation: Demonstrates automated code review and feedback addressing

gradeTrace.description: Deterministic trajectory scorer: minimizes/maximizes metrics, returns 0-1 verdicts

otelExport.description: OTLP/JSON trace renderer with deterministic sha256 IDs and GenAI semconv attributes
